name: Tutorial Prompt
description: A contextual UI hint that teaches controls, rules, or next steps when the player is likely to need them.
design_notes: Tutorial prompts should be timely, dismissible, and tied to demonstrated player need. Repeating the same hint too often creates noise; hiding it forever after one dismissal can abandon players who need a reminder later.
design_purpose: Teach controls and mechanics at the moment they become relevant.
Reduce early confusion without stopping play unnecessarily.
Support reminders when players return after a break or miss a lesson.
player_fantasy: The player feels guided without being treated as if they have lost control.

parameters.name: prompt_trigger_condition_id
parameters.description: Condition that causes the prompt to appear.

parameters.name: repeat_after_failures
parameters.description: Number of failed attempts before showing the prompt again.

parameters.name: minimum_visible_ms
parameters.description: Minimum time the prompt remains visible once shown.

parameters.name: input_glyph_update_mode
parameters.description: When the prompt updates displayed bindings.

edge_cases: Prompts should update immediately when input device or bindings change.
A prompt during combat, dialogue, or cutscene can obscure urgent information.
Dismissed prompts may need to return after long absence, repeated failure, or changed control scheme.
Localization can turn a short hint into multi-line text that overlaps HUD elements.
common_bugs: Prompt appears after the player has already completed the taught action.
Dismissal state is saved globally and hides the prompt for new save files.
Input glyphs show default bindings instead of remapped controls.
Repeated trigger conditions flicker the prompt every frame.
balancing_notes: Hints should reduce confusion, not solve puzzles or remove discovery unless the game opts into guided play.
Failure-based prompts are often less intrusive than showing every instruction preemptively.
accessibility_notes: Allow prompt duration, size, and repeat behavior to be adjusted.
Use text plus icons, and ensure prompts are compatible with screen reader or narration systems where supported.

implementation_notes.unity: Bind the UI to gameplay state through events or observable view models, not direct polling from presentation widgets; support UI Toolkit, uGUI, and input glyph updates from the active Input System scheme.
implementation_notes.godot: Drive Control nodes from a small view-model dictionary or signal payload, and keep input focus, localization, and scaling separate from gameplay state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use UMG or CommonUI widgets fed by gameplay events, with focus navigation, platform glyphs, and replicated-state boundaries handled outside widget animation code.
implementation_notes.web: Render the UI from normalized game state, keep input mapping and localization data outside component logic, and avoid making animation completion the source of truth.

example_games.title: Portal
example_games.note: Uses contextual teaching and signage to introduce mechanics.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses concise prompts to introduce movement abilities.
